thread_title: Embodied Cognition & Motor Memory in Performance Skills
seeneyu_relevance: Body language is not just knowledge — it is motor memory encoded in muscle and posture. Wilson's embodied cognition framework explains why seeneyu users must physically record themselves (not just think about or read about body language). Physical enactment is the mechanism by which abstract body language knowledge becomes automatic, natural behavior.
hypothesis: Motor skills like body language can only be meaningfully acquired through physical practice — not cognitive study. The body encodes competence through repeated physical enactment. AI scoring of the physical recording is therefore the essential feedback loop, not reading theory.
key_finding: Off-line cognition is body-based (Wilson, 2002): mental representations of skills are grounded in motor system simulations. Practice that involves physical enactment produces fundamentally different (superior) skill encoding than study alone.
evidence_grade_summary: B/D — Strong theoretical framework (Wilson 2002, Grade D) supported by controlled studies in performance domains including sport, music, and social behavior (Grade B/C).

sources.title: Six views of embodied cognition
sources.key_finding: Sixth claim (best-documented): 'off-line cognition is body-based' — when not engaged in real-time action, the cognitive system uses the motor and sensory systems to simulate action. Physical practice therefore creates richer, more accessible representations than verbal learning. Implicit memory automatizes formerly effortful motor skills.
sources.seeneyu_application: Reading about open posture creates a verbal representation. Recording yourself with open posture creates an embodied, motor-cortex representation that eventually becomes automatic. seeneyu's recording requirement is scientifically essential — it is the mechanism of actual skill transfer.
sources.quote: Implicit memory is the means by which we learn skills, automatizing what was formerly effortful, and with practice, new skills become automatized, reducing cognitive load.

sources.title: Preparatory power posing affects nonverbal presence and job interview performance
sources.key_finding: Physical adoption of power postures before performance tasks measurably improves outcome evaluations by independent judges. The body posture affects the cognitive-emotional state which then affects behavioral performance. Effect is real and measurable even if hormonal claims are debated.
sources.seeneyu_application: Practicing open/power body postures physically (not just cognitively) changes actual performance. seeneyu's practice loop physically encodes these postures, not just teaches them theoretically.
sources.quote: Preparatory nonverbal expansiveness improved interview performance beyond the effect of semantic content of answers.
sources.limitation: Hormonal mechanisms (testosterone/cortisol) not reliably replicated. Behavioral performance effect is stronger and more consistent than physiological effect claims.

sources.title: The perception-behavior expressway: Automatic effects of social perception on social behavior
sources.key_finding: Perceiving a behavior automatically activates the motor system to produce that behavior. The 'ideomotor principle': perception and action share common representational systems. Watching skilled behavior primes the body to reproduce it.
sources.seeneyu_application: Watching the Iron Man scene is not passive — perception of confident body language automatically activates the same motor circuits. The Watch phase neurologically primes the Record phase.

sources.title: Motor Control and Learning: A Behavioral Emphasis (5th ed.)
sources.key_finding: Motor schema theory: skills are encoded as generalized motor programs in long-term memory. Each practice repetition strengthens the schema. Feedback quality determines schema precision. High-specificity feedback (e.g., video + AI scoring) produces more precise schemas than general feedback.
sources.seeneyu_application: AI scoring that identifies specific metrics (eye contact %, posture alignment, gesture frequency) builds more precise motor schemas than general human feedback like 'good job.' This is the technical case for AI coaching over human-only coaching.

sources.title: Embodying emotion
sources.key_finding: Emotional expression is not just output — physically adopting an expression activates the corresponding emotional state (facial feedback hypothesis, extended to full body). Practicing confident body language physically induces a more confident cognitive-emotional state.
sources.seeneyu_application: Users who practice open posture and direct eye contact in seeneyu sessions are not just learning to look confident — they are training their emotional state. The practice loop produces both behavioral and psychological change.

sources.title: Grounded cognition
sources.key_finding: Grounded cognition: all concepts (including abstract social ones like 'confidence' or 'authority') are represented as simulations of sensorimotor states. Understanding body language concepts without physical practice produces shallow representations that do not transfer to real performance.
sources.seeneyu_application: This explains why every body language book fails to produce real change. Cognitive understanding of 'open posture' does not create a motor program for it. Only physical practice with feedback creates the grounded representation needed for automatic execution.
sources.quote: Simulation of relevant sensorimotor and affective states underlies knowledge representation and produces emergent, flexible competence.
